items.title: Alberta to unveil details of 'million-barrel-per-day' oil pipeline route to West Coast on July 2
items.summary_auto: The Alberta government says it plans to unveil details Thursday about a proposed "one-million-barrel-per-day oil pipeline" through British Columbia.

items.title: Social media sites pushing self-harm content, says mother of B.C. teen who died
items.summary_auto: The grieving mother from British Columbia has issued an emotional call for change, including a push for the regulation of self-harm and suicide-promoting content targeting minors, and independent oversight of social media platforms.

items.title: 'It took 10 seconds to do this': Manitoba retirees lose home to tornado
items.summary_auto: Bernadine and Brian Brown's home in Rossburn was destroyed after a tornado swept through the western Manitoba community on the weekend. The retirees were rummaging through the debris to see what they could salvage Monday.

items.title: Canadians exposed to hantavirus on cruise ship complete isolation period
items.summary_auto: All Canadians who were exposed to the Andes hantavirus earlier this year linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship have finished their self-isolation period, Health Canada and the Public Health Agency of Canada said on social media Monday.

items.title: Judiciary must listen to ordinary people, says Supreme Court nominee Glenn Joyal
items.summary_auto: Canada's newest Supreme Court nominee believes that better outreach and communication of the work of the judiciary can restore trust in the institution at a time when judges are under increasing scrutiny.

items.title: Stephen Eustáquio's brother says it was 'unreal' watching him make World Cup history
items.summary_auto: Mauro Eustáquio watched his little brother make history on Sunday when he scored the game-winning goal for Canada against South Africa at the FIFA World Cup 2026.

items.title: Sudan declares new cholera outbreak months after official end to one that lasted nearly 2 years
items.summary_auto: Sudan has declared a new outbreak of cholera, the country's Ministry of Heath reported Monday, with 117 deaths in West Kordofan state, another seven confirmed cases and 838 suspected cases.

items.title: Body of missing inmate found in waters off Vancouver Island
items.summary_auto: Correctional Service Canada (CSC) says an inmate who was declared missing from a seaside minimum-security prison on Vancouver Island is dead.

items.title: Residents still in Fort Simpson, N.W.T., ordered to shelter in place due to wildfire
Poilievre set to shuffle Conservative Opposition critics: sources
items.summary_auto: Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre is set to announce a shuffling of his critic roles and front bench, according to Conservative sources, as he looks to adapt his messaging to Prime Minister Mark Carney's majority government.

items.title: Late-night host John Oliver is fulfilling his lifelong dream ... of becoming a soap opera star
items.summary_auto: Even someone as accomplished as comedian and late-night host John Oliver can still harbour a desire for something more. And come the beginning of July, he’ll finally be able to say he’s achieved it: his lifelong dream … of starring in a daytime soap opera.

items.title: Palestinian exhibit at human rights museum 'should be rectified': federal heritage minister
items.summary_auto: Heritage Minister Marc Miller said Monday the Canadian Museum for Human Rights in Winnipeg has erred in how it curated an exhibit about displaced Palestinians.

items.title: Tariff deal with Trump unlikely before U.S. midterms, says Canada's former trade chief
items.summary_auto: Canada's former chief trade negotiator says he doesn't expect Ottawa to reach a tariff deal with Washington before the U.S. midterm elections.

items.title: Ontario and Utah sign deal to boost trade as Ford seeks to strengthen ties with American states
items.summary_auto: Ontario has signed a deal with Utah to boost trade as part of a plan by Premier Doug Ford to strengthen economic ties with individual American states.

items.title: Inside the World Cup's international police command centre
items.summary_auto: CBC News got rare access inside the International Police Cooperation Center in Virginia, where law enforcement agencies from around the world are coordinating security for the FIFA World Cup, including monitoring drone threats and sharing intelligence.

items.title: Suspects charged after N.S. couple confronts car thieves to rescue stolen dog
items.summary_auto: Three people are facing charges following the June 11 incident in Pictou County.

items.title: Michael becomes the highest-grossing biopic of all time — despite criticisms against limited scope
items.summary_auto: Despite middling reviews, strong competition and even late rewrites and legal hurdles, the Jackson family film Michael has become the highest-grossing biopic of all time.

items.title: Is Canada's 'technical recession' already over?
items.summary_auto: Is Canada's so-called "technical recession" already over? Canada's economy likely grew by 0.4 per cent in April.

items.title: Has Canada's GDP bounced back?
items.summary_auto: Is Canada's so-called "technical recession" already over? Canada's economy likely grew by 0.4 per cent in April.

items.title: Akwesasne man pleads guilty in smuggling case that left family with 2 toddlers dead
items.summary_auto: A 31-year-old man pleaded guilty last week for his role in a human smuggling conspiracy that led to the 2023 drowning deaths of a family, including two children under the age of three, in the St. Lawrence River.

items.title: Recovery effort underway for 4 men, 2 women presumed drowned off B.C. coast: RCMP
Murder-suicide leaves 2 boys and their father dead, Ottawa police say
items.summary_auto: Ottawa police say they are investigating a murder-suicide that has left two children and their father dead.

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court rejects Trump-led challenge against mail-in ballots
items.summary_auto: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count ballots that arrive after election day, a persistent target of President Donald Trump.

items.title: Petition signature counting resumes as Alberta separatist group gets partial win at court of appeal
items.summary_auto: An Alberta separatist group has secured a partial win at the province’s top court after a lower court judge quashed its referendum petition last month.

items.title: Italy, Balkans latest in Europe to feel brunt of record-breaking heat wave
items.summary_auto: Italy and the Balkans felt the impact on Monday of a record-breaking heat wave that has caused hundreds of excess deaths and disrupted daily life across the continent for more than a week, with growing concerns over the ‌spread of wildfires.

items.title: Forest fire prompts evacuation orders for Walsh River, other areas near Labrador City
items.summary_auto: An evacuation order has been issued for the area of Walsh River and other locations, near Labrador City.

items.title: Venezuelans race against clock looking for earthquake survivors
items.summary_auto: Five days have passed since twin earthquakes struck northern Venezuela, killing more than 1,450 people, according to the government. Relief organizations say the first 72 hours after a natural disaster is the most crucial time period for rescues, though survival can be extended if people have access to food and water.
